programme.title: Saturdays In the South: A History of SEC Football
programme.desc: Multiple SEC schools claim national titles from the 1930s through the '50s, including LSU with star running back Billy Cannon in 1958, prior to Cannon's epic game-winning punt return against powerhouse Ole Miss a year later.

programme.title: Saturdays In the South: A History of SEC Football
programme.desc: In the 1960s, as Georgia Tech leaves the conference and the Civil Rights Movement becomes embedded in the history and image of the region, Bear Bryant, hired in 1958, becomes the face of Alabama - and SEC football.

programme.title: Saturdays In the South: A History of SEC Football
programme.desc: As a new decade dawns, quarterbacks Archie Manning, Bert Jones and Condredge Holloway build memorable legacies. Bear Bryant begins a new run of dominance at Alabama, and the rise of television sports transforms the SEC.

programme.title: Saturdays In the South: A History of SEC Football
programme.desc: The 1980s begin with Herschel Walker leading Georgia to a national title as a freshman. After Bear Bryant retires in 1982 and passes away four weeks later, control of the SEC is eventually transferred to Pat Dye and Bo Jackson at Auburn.

programme.title: Saturdays In the South: A History of SEC Football
programme.desc: The 1990s bring change, with expansion and the sport's first-ever conference championship game. Florida's Steve Spurrier fashions an era of high-octane offense, and his Gators form a rivalry with Tennessee and QB Peyton Manning.

programme.title: TrueSouth
programme.desc: Greek natives and their kin built the Birmingham restaurant scene, from barbecue joints to hot dog stands to steakhouses; Bright Star, founded in 1907, is the old guard and Johnny's is the new guard Greek restaurant.

programme.title: TrueSouth
programme.desc: Pablo Rivadeneyra rotisserie cooks lime-marinated chicken; Larry and Betty Jean Scott work a smoke-belching pit at Scott's BBQ; in these two places, we get a glimpse of the hard work that defines life in the South.

programme.title: Live: Unsportsmanlike with Evan, Canty and Michelle
programme.desc: Start the day off right with Chris Canty, Evan Cohen and Michelle Smallmon providing an energizing and informative way to take on the day. Sports, life and laughter are what it's all about.

programme.title: Saturdays In the South: A History of SEC Football
programme.desc: Nick Saban comes to LSU in 2000 and eventually wins the Tigers' first national championship since 1958 - a feat matched by Les Miles. At Florida, Tim Tebow becomes a cult hero and wins two national titles with Urban Meyer's Gators.

programme.title: Saturdays In the South: A History of SEC Football
programme.desc: The SEC has a run of seven straight national titles, and nine in 12 years. Expansion in 2012 gives the conference 14 schools. Meanwhile, Nick Saban arguably becomes the greatest coach in the history of college football.

programme.title: SEC Storied
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of ex-NFL star Steve Spurrier, one of the most fascinating and competitive men in college football.

programme.title: The Draft: Featured
programme.desc: Highlights of top draft prospects in their college football careers.

programme.title: SEC Now
programme.desc: News & information show hosted by either Peter Burns, Dari Nowkhah or Alyssa Lang, joined by various network analysts, discussing all the SEC news of the day.

programme.title: TrueSouth
programme.desc: Sands, the soul food café, cooks oxtails and cabbage and ropy okra; Arnold's Country Kitchen, Kahlil Arnold dishes created corn, hashed turnip greens, and fried green tomatoes; the differences between black and white cooking come into focus.

programme.title: SEC Storied
programme.desc: ESPN's original documentaries offer a wide range of compelling stories from the world of sports. These documentaries delve into the lives of athletes, explore iconic moments, and examine the cultural impact of sports.

programme.title: SEC Storied
programme.desc: The story of Bayler Teal, Charlie Peters and the 2010-2011 South Carolina championship baseball teams.

programme.title: SEC Storied
programme.desc: When Skip Bertman arrives in Baton Rouge, La., in the summer of 1983 as LSU's new head baseball coach, the program is mired in mediocrity; from those beginnings, he turns the Tigers into a powerhouse, winning five national titles in a 10-year span.

programme.title: Hooked on Africa
programme.desc: The unspoilt wilderness of South Africa's Eastern Cape coastline takes you back to when time was measured by the tides and little else mattered, except the lure of the myriad species to be found off the rocks and in the abundant estuaries.

programme.title: Hooked on Travel
programme.desc: The estuaries of Angola are the nursery for all fish that roam off its cold Atlantic seaboard, and it's where in certain seasons the Jacks and Tarpon are to be found. Tommo and Wes split up to experiment in two of Angola's most prolific waterways.

programme.title: Reel Africa
programme.desc: The coastline surrounding Port Elizabeth is part of the beautiful Wild Coast and is a preferred fishing destination for many an angler.

programme.title: Inside Angling
programme.desc: The upper section of the Zambezi River, while difficult to get to, never fails to deliver a fantastic fishing experience. We go in search of Africa's Piranha and come face to face with the business end of this freshwater monster.

programme.title: Wild Fly
programme.desc: Few wilderness destinations compare to the mighty Zambezi, where ferocious tigerfish are found in crocodile infested waters. The Wildfly Team experience an incredible journey in amongst Africa's wildlife.
